topic: Area of Study 1: Western Classical Tradition 1650-1910
subject: music

items.q: Are Baroque, Classical and Romantic just interchangeable words for old music?
items.correct: No - they are distinct eras with different features: continuo and ornamentation, then balanced clarity, then expanded expressive extremes.
items.misconception: All orchestral music from 1650 to 1910 is one single style.
items.random_wrong: The three words describe three different countries.
items.why: Placing a piece in the right era from its features is the core skill of this area of study; merging the eras makes stylistic comparison impossible.

items.q: Did Haydn invent the symphony out of nothing?
items.correct: He did not invent it single-handedly, but he standardised and perfected the four-movement symphony, earning the nickname 'father of the symphony'.
items.misconception: Before Haydn there were no symphonies at all.
items.random_wrong: Haydn wrote only one symphony in his life.
items.why: The 'father of' nickname misleads students into thinking invention rather than development; early symphonies existed, and Haydn's 104 refined the genre.

items.q: Is the recapitulation in sonata form an exact repeat of the exposition?
items.correct: No - the second subject returns in the tonic key instead of the dominant, resolving the movement's tonal tension.
items.misconception: The recapitulation copies the exposition note for note.
items.random_wrong: The recapitulation is played by the percussion section alone.
items.why: The key change is the structural point of the recapitulation; missing it means missing how sonata form creates departure and homecoming.
Correct answer: Beethoven. “Mozart” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: 104. “41” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What three notes form a major triad in root position?
items.misconception: Dorian has no sharps or flats at all.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Root, major third, perfect fifth.. “Dorian has no sharps or flats at all.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What does modulation mean in music?
items.correct: Changing from one key to another within a piece.
items.why: It's Changing from one key to another within a piece.. “Adjusting volume electronically.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which of these is the best definition of “Joseph Haydn”?
items.correct: 1732-1809; 'father of the symphony and string quartet'; 104 symphonies.
items.misconception: Three-part structure: exposition, development, recapitulation; basis of first movements.
items.why: It's 1732-1809; 'father of the symphony and string quartet'; 104 symphonies.. “Three-part structure: exposition, development, recapitulation; basis of first movements.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which of these is the best definition of “Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart”?
items.misconception: Multi-movement orchestral work, typically four movements.
items.random_wrong: 1732-1809; 'father of the symphony and string quartet'; 104 symphonies.
items.why: Correct answer: 1756-1791; Austrian prodigy; 41 symphonies, operas (e.g., 'Magic Flute').. “Multi-movement orchestral work, typically four movements.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which of these is the best definition of “Classical era”?
items.correct: ~1750-1820; clarity, balance, sonata form; orchestra expanded.
items.misconception: 1770-1827; bridged Classical and Romantic; 9 symphonies including the 5th and 9th ('Choral').
items.random_wrong: Multi-movement orchestral work, typically four movements.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: ~1750-1820; clarity, balance, sonata form; orchestra expanded.. “1770-1827; bridged Classical and Romantic; 9 symphonies including the 5th and 9th ('Choral').” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: 9 symphonies. “10 symphonies” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
